reasoning: Elon Musk made a $97.4 billion unsolicited hostile takeover bid for OpenAI in February 2025, giving a deadline of May 10, 2025. OpenAI's board unanimously rejected the offer just days later in February, referring to it as “not a bid at all.” Over the ensuing months, Musk and OpenAI engaged in continued legal battles. Additionally, in May 2025, OpenAI chose to scrap its plans to convert to a for-profit structure, but Musk has stated his lawsuit will proceed anyway. Because the bid was rejected outright, the May 10 deadline has passed, and litigation is ongoing, there is virtually zero chance Musk and OpenAI will formally agree to a process for him to purchase or control the company by August 31, 2025. The market estimate of 0.02 aligns perfectly with this.
